payload.topic:
FUNDAMENTAL QUESTION: AGENT IDENTITY GRANULARITY AND DYNAMIC CAPABILITY COMPOSITION

## THE CORE TENSION

HestAI's Odyssean Anchor was originally designed around compositional identity:
  ROLE = SHANK (cognition/how you think) + ARM (phase context/where you operate) + FLUKES (capabilities/what you can do)

The original system had ~3-5 base agents (LOGOS, ETHOS, PATHOS) that became many roles by swapping ARMs. LOGOS + BUILD_ARM = implementation-lead. LOGOS + DESIGN_ARM = design-architect. The ARM determined which FLUKES loaded. Clean, composable, linear scaling.

The current system evolved into 30+ specialized agents, each with a fixed identity and 1-2 profiles. The v8.1 chassis-profile system IS the ARM/FLUKES concept — but we've frozen the ARM into each agent's identity rather than keeping it dynamic.

## EVIDENCE OF THE PROBLEM

During systematic agent interviews (batch 1 of 8):
- 16 of 30 agents share LOGOS cognition (same SHANK)
- 50-80% of declared skills per agent are phantom references (skills that don't exist)
- Multiple agents cover overlapping domains (CRS/CE/quality-observer all do validation)
- The phantom epidemic suggests over-specialization — each niche identity demands unique skills nobody wrote
- A solo developer must remember 30 agent names and when to invoke each

## THE QUESTION

What is the right granularity of agent identity for a solo-developer AI orchestration system?

Options to consider (and transcend):

A) CONSOLIDATION: Reduce to ~8-10 agents with rich profile sets. Group by cognition:
   - LOGOS-BUILDER (impl-lead, completion-architect, task-decomposer)
   - ETHOS-VALIDATOR (critical-engineer, CRS, quality-observer)
   - LOGOS-ARCHITECT (technical-architect, design-architect)
   Each has multiple profiles loading different skill sets per context.

B) KEEP 30 SPECIALIZED: Current approach. High fidelity per role but high maintenance.

C) DYNAMIC ARM COMPOSITION: Return to the original OA vision. Few base agents, context determines capabilities. The ARM isn't a static profile — it's computed from the task context.

D) SOMETHING ELSE ENTIRELY: What has nobody proposed?

## KEY CONSTRAINTS
- Solo developer system — no team coordination overhead
- The anchor ceremony already dynamically loads capabilities based on context
- Agent files are consumed by Odyssean Anchor which reads §3::CAPABILITIES
- Skills can be loaded as full-body or kernel_only depending on profile
- The system must remain Git-auditable and hermetically self-contained
- Behavioral fidelity matters most — agents must act consistently within their role constraints

## WHAT MUST BE PRESERVED
- The SEA (constitution/principles) — stable, always loaded
- The SHANK (cognition) — determines HOW the agent thinks
- The ability to BLOCK — some agents must be able to stop work
- Git auditability of agent identity
- The holographic contract — an agent file should be self-contained DNA

## WHAT CAN CHANGE
- The number of agents
- How capabilities are mapped to agents
- How profiles/ARMs are defined and activated
- The relationship between agent identity and skill loading
- The anchor ceremony's resolution mechanism

## REFERENCE FILES (agents should read these for full context)
- Original OA Guide: /Volumes/HestAI-old/hestai-orchestrator/docs/guides/ODYSSEAN_ANCHOR_GUIDE.md
- Current agents spec: /Volumes/HestAI-MCP/worktrees/agent-interviews/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/octave_mcp/resources/specs/octave-agents-spec.oct.md
- Current skills spec: /Volumes/HestAI-MCP/worktrees/agent-interviews/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/octave_mcp/resources/specs/octave-skills-spec.oct.md
- Example consolidated agent (HO v8.1): /Volumes/HestAI-MCP/worktrees/agent-interviews/src/hestai_mcp/_bundled_hub/library/agents/holistic-orchestrator.oct.md
- Example specialized agent (CRS v8.1): /Volumes/HestAI-MCP/worktrees/agent-interviews/src/hestai_mcp/_bundled_hub/library/agents/code-review-specialist.oct.md
- Example specialized agent (complexity-guard v8.1): /Volumes/HestAI-MCP/worktrees/agent-interviews/src/hestai_mcp/_bundled_hub/library/agents/complexity-guard.oct.md
- Cognition files: /Volumes/HestAI-MCP/worktrees/agent-interviews/.hestai-sys/library/cognitions/logos.oct.md, ethos.oct.md, pathos.oct.md
- Skills-expert (creation-first agent): /Volumes/HestAI-MCP/worktrees/agent-interviews/src/hestai_mcp/_bundled_hub/library/agents/skills-expert.oct.md
- Agent-expert (architecture authority): /Volumes/HestAI-MCP/worktrees/agent-interviews/src/hestai_mcp/_bundled_hub/library/agents/agent-expert.oct.md
- Constitution: /Volumes/HestAI-MCP/worktrees/agent-interviews/.hestai-sys/CONSTITUTION.md
- North Star: /Volumes/HestAI-MCP/worktrees/agent-interviews/.hestai/north-star/000-MCP-PRODUCT-NORTH-STAR-SUMMARY.oct.md
- Previous debate on skills management: /Volumes/HestAI-MCP/worktrees/agent-interviews/debates/2026-03-21-skills-management-architecture-01km8yc4.events.jsonl
